---
title: "VA_Picrust_differential_abundance"
author: "Emily"
date: "2026-03-17"
output: html_document
---
  
```{r libraries, include=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(phyloseq)
library(ggpicrust2)
library(Maaslin2)
library(pheatmap)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:
  
```{r}
metadata = read.csv("../../../Datasets/Metadata/parkinsons_metadata_filt_cat.csv", row.names = 1)
metadata_sub = metadata %>% filter(Tert_VA %in% c("VA Low", "VA High")) %>%
  mutate(Tert_VA = fct_drop(as.factor(Tert_VA)))
```

ggpicrust2 to run "Maaslin2", another common differential abundance tool, on your groups of interest and add pathway annotations
```{r}
meta = readRDS('../../../Datasets/phyloseq_taxonomy.rds') %>% .@sam_data %>% data.frame() %>% 
  rownames_to_column('sample_name')

pathway_abundance = read.delim('../../../QIIME2_outputs/path_abun_unstrat.tsv',row.names = 1)

# Filter
path_filt = pathway_abundance %>% select(all_of(rownames(metadata_sub)))


diff_abun = pathway_daa(abundance = path_filt,
                        metadata = metadata,
                        group = "Tert_VA",
                        daa_method = "Maaslin2",
                        fixed_effects = c("Sex", "Age", "entacapone", "Energy_kcal"),
                        select = NULL,
                        reference = "VA Low")

daa_annotated_results_df = pathway_annotation(pathway = "MetaCyc",
                                              daa_results_df = diff_abun,
                                              ko_to_kegg = FALSE)

# save
saveRDS(daa_annotated_results_df,'daa_annotated_results_df.rds')

readRDS('daa_annotated_results_df.rds')

```
Plots
```{r}
source('../ggpicrust2_errorbar_function_fixed.R')

# Filter out NA in p-values and log2 fold change columns
daa_annotated_results_df_filt = daa_annotated_results_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(p_adjust), !is.na(log2_fold_change))

# Adjusted P-value = 17 hits
daa_annotated_results_df_filt %>% 
  filter(p_adjust < 0.005) %>% 
  nrow()

# Log2 Fold Change values = 8 hits (sufficient for plot)
daa_annotated_results_df_filt %>% 
  filter(abs(log2_fold_change) >= 4e-3) %>% 
  nrow()

daa_annotated_results_df_filt %>% filter(p_adjust< 0.005, abs(log2_fold_change) > 4e-3) %>% nrow()

# Dataframe for plotting
daa_plot <- daa_annotated_results_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(p_adjust), !is.na(log2_fold_change)) %>%
  filter(abs(log2_fold_change) >= 4e-3)

nrow(daa_plot)

peb <- pathway_errorbar_fixed(abundance = path_filt,
                              daa_results_df = daa_plot,
                              Group = metadata_sub$Tert_VA,
                              wrap_label = TRUE,wraplength = 60,
                              fc_cutoff = 4e-3,order_by_log = TRUE,
                              p_values_threshold = 1,
                              order = "name",
                              ko_to_kegg = FALSE,
                              p_value_bar = TRUE,
                              x_lab = "description")

library(patchwork)

# Add tags A and B automatically
peb_final <- peb + plot_annotation(tag_levels = 'A')

# View the result
peb_final

# Save the final version with the tags
ggsave("VA-picrust-final-with-labels.png", plot = peb_final, width = 12, height = 7, dpi = 300)


ggsave("VA-picrust-sig-pathways.png",
       plot = peb, width = 18, height = 10)
```

Heatmap

```{r identical features}
sig_features <- daa_annotated_results_df %>% 
  filter(!is.na(p_adjust), !is.na(log2_fold_change)) %>%
  filter(abs(log2_fold_change) >= 4e-3) %>% 
  pull(feature) %>% 
  unique()

path_relab = path_filt %>% 
  apply(2,function(x) x/sum(x)) %>% as.data.frame()
colSums(path_relab) # Should be all 1

stats = path_relab %>% t() %>%  # switch rows and columns
  as.data.frame() %>% # Lets us use select()
  select(all_of(sig_features)) %>% 
  cor(method = 'pearson') #Pearson tests between every feature pair

# Define the color palette
color_palette <- colorRampPalette(c("blue", "white", "red"))(40)

# Define breaks for the color scale
breaks <- seq(-1, 1, length.out = 41)  # 40 colors + 1 for the endpoint

# Lookup Names
name_lookup <- daa_annotated_results_df %>%
  filter(feature %in% sig_features) %>%
  select(feature, description) %>% # Adjust 'description' to your actual column name
  distinct()

readable_names <- name_lookup$description[match(rownames(stats), name_lookup$feature)]
rownames(stats) <- readable_names
colnames(stats) <- readable_names

view(readable_names)

# Create the clustered heatmap with centered color at zero
# install.packages("pheatmap")
library('pheatmap')
pheatmap(stats, 
         clustering_distance_rows = "euclidean",  
         clustering_distance_cols = "euclidean", 
         clustering_method = "complete",          
         color = color_palette, 
         breaks = breaks,
         border_color = "white",       # Clean white borders between cells
         display_numbers = TRUE,       # Optional: shows the actual R values
         number_color = "black",
         fontsize_row = 12,            # Slightly larger for readability
         fontsize_col = 12,
         angle_col = 45,               # Tilt labels so they don't overlap
         main = '',
         filename = "../../../Results/Figures/Aim_3/MetaCyc-VA_Heatmap_Final.png",
         height = 8, width = 10)       # Wider to accommodate long names


# to try to minimize white space
pheatmap(stats, 
         clustering_distance_rows = "euclidean",  
         clustering_distance_cols = "euclidean", 
         clustering_method = "complete",          
         color = color_palette, 
         breaks = breaks,
         border_color = "white",
         display_numbers = TRUE,
         number_format = "%.2f",      
         fontsize_row = 10,
         fontsize_col = 10,
         angle_col = 315,              # rotated to minimize white space
         treeheight_row = 25,         
         treeheight_col = 25, 
         legend = TRUE,               
         main = NA,                   # Use NA to properly suppress the title space
         filename = "../../../Results/Figures/Aim_3/MetaCyc-VA_Heatmap_Final-new.png",
         width = 8,                   # Increased slightly for your long pathway names
         height = 6)

```
See how similar your sample groups are

```{r PCA}
# Generate pathway PCA plot
pathway_pca(abundance = path_filt,
            metadata = metadata_sub, 
            group = "Tert_VA")
```
